libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Do not use org.apache.pekko.http.ccompat.pre213 or since213: those classes existed in 1.3.0 but are absent from 2.0.0-M1. Focus tests on org.apache.pekko.macros.LogHelper and LogHelperMacro. Because LogHelper is private[pekko], direct Scala tests that implement or call it should be in package org.apache.pekko or a subpackage.

deterministic_setup.reason: pekko-parsing_2.13:2.0.0-M1 declares pekko-actor_2.13 as provided, and LogHelper references org.apache.pekko.event.LoggingAdapter and org.apache.pekko.annotation.InternalApi from that artifact.
pekko-parsing_2.13:2.0.0-M1 declares scala-reflect as provided, and LogHelperMacro uses scala.reflect.macros.blackbox and related reflection APIs.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8878 [Automation] java run fails for org.apache.pekko:pekko-parsing_2.13:2.0.0-M1; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: The artifact is an internal support module with very small public surface, so coverage may be limited to macro class loading or carefully packaged Scala macro usage.
If the generated test tries to preserve the old ccompat macro-annotation coverage, compilation will fail because those classes were removed in 2.0.0-M1.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: The resolved 2.0.0-M1 artifact only contains internal LogHelper macro classes and declares pekko-actor_2.13 and scala-reflect as provided dependencies; those are needed for meaningful classloading or macro coverage.
